samples.video_title: Jim Jordan reacts to Eric Swalwell's resignation | Katie Pavlich Tonight

groups.source_text: Boasberg said. He said, turn the plane around. Bring the bad guys back. Back to the United States. I mean, these are people who are in our country illegally who did terrible things, and we were moving them back, repatriating them back to their. To their, uh, home country. And the judge said, turn the plane around, bring him back to the country. That is the most ridiculous thing I've ever heard. And of course, as. As most Americans were rightfully concerned about that, one of the things we did is said, okay, if a. One of the federal district judges, we passed this bill out of our committee. Now the House, if one of our district judges is making a decision, it shouldn't have nationwide implications. If they issue an injunction, it should apply only to the parties in the case. In that respective jurisdiction as a way to kind of curtail some of the things we are seeing from these left wing activist judges.

House Democrats, led by Jamie Raskin, who of course led, uh, the impeachments against President Trump, has just unveiled a bill to examine removing Trump using the 25th amendment. Is this going anywhere given, uh, that the cabinet would have to be involved, or is this just another show from Democrats?

No, and it's ridiculous and everyone knows it's ridiculous. And, and frankly, when you think about what Democrats said and did for almost the entire term of President Biden, I mean, I had someone say to me, oh, well, you guys went after President Biden. No, we didn't. The first one to really go after President Biden was Robert Hur. Not until Robert Hur came forward. The special counsel that the Biden Justice Department appointed to look into President Biden's mishandling of classified information. He's the one who said Joe Biden would be difficult to prosecute because he would come across as a sympathetic older gentleman who can't remember things. That was Robert Hur who cited this concern. And yet they defended the President Obama for four years. And now they come some ridiculous claim that they're going to go after President Trump. This is again just Democrats with their endless, constant attack on President Trump, which frankly we have seen since clear back in 2016.

Let's turn to the election now. Midterm Elections ActBlue raised $568 million in the first quarter of 2026. They set a record for a midterm election year in this first quarter. This, of course, has Republicans in the Trump administration investigating them over allegations that allowed foreign donors to illegally contribute. There was a huge story in the New York Times recently about this. What is happening here, uh, to hold the group accountable. And if there are allegations of foreign donations and they're making record money in an election year, seems like that might be a problem ahead of November.

Well, we deposed them. Let's just say this, that they didn't say a whole lot. So we're looking into this. But the most recent news is their, their CEO, Ms. Wallace Jones, had responded to a letter from Chairman Steil Republican, uh, chairman of the House Administration Committee. And in that letter she said some things that, that looked like they weren't accurate relative to what they did concerning foreign contributions. So much so that their counsel said, hey, we think you may have misrepresented and misled the Congress. And that lawyer stepped down. And by the way, that lawyer was a key lawyer in the Obama administration. Dana Remus who worked at the big law firm of Covington. So that's how serious it's getting. We're going to keep investigating and digging into.

reference_translations.source_text: Boasberg said. He said, turn the plane around. Bring the bad guys back. Back to the United States. I mean, these are people who are in our country illegally who did terrible things, and we were moving them back, repatriating them back to their. To their, uh, home country. And the judge said, turn the plane around, bring him back to the country. That is the most ridiculous thing I've ever heard. And of course, as. As most Americans were rightfully concerned about that, one of the things we did is said, okay, if a. One of the federal district judges, we passed this bill out of our committee. Now the House, if one of our district judges is making a decision, it shouldn't have nationwide implications. If they issue an injunction, it should apply only to the parties in the case. In that respective jurisdiction as a way to kind of curtail some of the things we are seeing from these left wing activist judges.
reference_translations.cn_text: 博斯伯格说，让飞机掉头，把坏人带回美国。我是说，这些人是非法留在我们国家的人，做了可怕的事，我们正把他们遣返回祖国。而法官却说，让飞机掉头把他们带回来。这是我听过最荒谬的事。大多数美国人理所当然对此感到担忧，所以我们在委员会通过了一项法案。现在众议院提出，如果一位联邦地区法官做出决定或发布禁令，它不应该产生全国性的影响，而只应适用于各自管辖区内的案件当事人。以此来限制那些左翼激进法官的作为。

reference_translations.source_text: House Democrats, led by Jamie Raskin, who of course led, uh, the impeachments against President Trump, has just unveiled a bill to examine removing Trump using the 25th amendment. Is this going anywhere given, uh, that the cabinet would have to be involved, or is this just another show from Democrats?
reference_translations.cn_text: 由杰米·拉斯金领导的众议院民主党人，他曾领导了，呃，针对特朗普总统的弹劾，刚公布了一项法案，探讨利用第25条修正案罢免特朗普。考虑到，呃，内阁必须参与其中，这会有什么结果吗，或者这只是民主党人的另一场作秀？

reference_translations.source_text: No, and it's ridiculous and everyone knows it's ridiculous. And, and frankly, when you think about what Democrats said and did for almost the entire term of President Biden, I mean, I had someone say to me, oh, well, you guys went after President Biden. No, we didn't. The first one to really go after President Biden was Robert Hur. Not until Robert Hur came forward. The special counsel that the Biden Justice Department appointed to look into President Biden's mishandling of classified information. He's the one who said Joe Biden would be difficult to prosecute because he would come across as a sympathetic older gentleman who can't remember things. That was Robert Hur who cited this concern. And yet they defended the President Obama for four years. And now they come some ridiculous claim that they're going to go after President Trump. This is again just Democrats with their endless, constant attack on President Trump, which frankly we have seen since clear back in 2016.
reference_translations.cn_text: 不，这很荒谬，大家都知道这有多荒谬。坦白说，想想民主党在几乎拜登整个任期内的所作所为。有人曾对我说，哦，是你们在针对拜登，不，我们没有。第一个真正针对拜登的是罗伯特·许。这位拜登司法部任命的特别顾问，负责调查他处理机密信息不当的问题。是他说拜登很难被起诉，因为他像个记不清事情、惹人同情的老绅士。正是罗伯特·许提出了这个担忧。然而他们却为奥巴马总统辩护了四年。现在又荒谬地声称要针对特朗普总统。这又是民主党对特朗普无休止的、持续不断的攻击，坦白说，早在2016年起我们就一直看到现在。

reference_translations.source_text: Let's turn to the election now. Midterm Elections ActBlue raised $568 million in the first quarter of 2026. They set a record for a midterm election year in this first quarter. This, of course, has Republicans in the Trump administration investigating them over allegations that allowed foreign donors to illegally contribute. There was a huge story in the New York Times recently about this. What is happening here, uh, to hold the group accountable. And if there are allegations of foreign donations and they're making record money in an election year, seems like that might be a problem ahead of November.
reference_translations.cn_text: 现在转向选举。ActBlue在2026年一季度筹集了5.68亿美元，创下中期选举年首季纪录。这促使特朗普政府的共和党人对其展开调查，指控其允许外国非法捐款。《纽约时报》近期对此做了大篇幅报道。该如何追究该组织的责任？若面临外国捐款指控，又在选举年筹得创纪录资金，这在11月前恐怕会是个问题。

reference_translations.source_text: Well, we deposed them. Let's just say this, that they didn't say a whole lot. So we're looking into this. But the most recent news is their, their CEO, Ms. Wallace Jones, had responded to a letter from Chairman Steil Republican, uh, chairman of the House Administration Committee. And in that letter she said some things that, that looked like they weren't accurate relative to what they did concerning foreign contributions. So much so that their counsel said, hey, we think you may have misrepresented and misled the Congress. And that lawyer stepped down. And by the way, that lawyer was a key lawyer in the Obama administration. Dana Remus who worked at the big law firm of Covington. So that's how serious it's getting. We're going to keep investigating and digging into.
reference_translations.cn_text: 我们对他们进行了取证。这么说吧，他们没透露太多，所以我们正在调查。最新消息是，其首席执行官华莱士·琼斯女士，回复了众议院行政委员会主席、共和党人斯泰尔的信。信中她关于外国捐款的说法，似乎与事实不符。以至于他们的律师说，我们认为你可能歪曲事实并误导了国会。随后该律师辞职了。顺便一提，达娜·雷穆斯曾是奥巴马政府的关键律师，在科文顿大律所工作。可见事态有多严重。我们将继续深入调查。

samples.translation_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Jim Jordan reacts to Eric Swalwell's resignation | Katie Pavlich Tonight
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KpW50xQGoAU

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
Eric Swalwell → 埃里克·斯瓦尔韦尔
James Boasberg → 詹姆斯·博斯伯格
Kristi Noem → 克里斯蒂·诺姆
DHS → 美国国土安全部
Jamie Raskin → 杰米·拉斯金
Robert Hur → 罗伯特·许
ActBlue → ActBlue
Bryan Steil → 布莱恩·斯泰尔
Dana Remus → 达娜·雷穆斯
Covington → 科文顿律师事务所
NewsNation → NewsNation

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

rewrite_cases.rewrite_prompt:
你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。

任务：对当前文本进行缩短，使其更适合目标配音时长。

当前文本（180字）：
博斯伯格说，让飞机掉头，把坏人带回美国。我是说，这些人是非法留在我们国家的人，做了可怕的事，我们正把他们遣返回祖国。而法官却说，让飞机掉头把他们带回来。这是我听过最荒谬的事。大多数美国人理所当然对此感到担忧，所以我们在委员会通过了一项法案。现在众议院提出，如果一位联邦地区法官做出决定或发布禁令，它不应该产生全国性的影响，而只应适用于各自管辖区内的案件当事人。以此来限制那些左翼激进法官的作为。

英文原文（参考，不要直接翻译）：
Boasberg said. He said, turn the plane around. Bring the bad guys back. Back to the United States. I mean, these are people who are in our country illegally who did terrible things, and we were moving them back, repatriating them back to their. To their, uh, home country. And the judge said, turn the plane around, bring him back to the country. That is the most ridiculous thing I've ever heard. And of course, as. As most Americans were rightfully concerned about that, one of the things we did is said, okay, if a. One of the federal district judges, we passed this bill out of our committee. Now the House, if one of our district judges is making a decision, it shouldn't have nationwide implications. If they issue an injunction, it should apply only to the parties in the case. In that respective jurisdiction as a way to kind of curtail some of the things we are seeing from these left wing activist judges.

目标字数：约191字（建议控制在171~210字之间）
目标时长区间：尽量落在目标时长的90%~110%
当前需要缩短约0%

要求：
1. 保持原意不变，不要增删核心信息
2. 删减冗余词汇、连接词，精简表达
3. 保持自然口语化，适合视频配音
4. 参考英文原文确保核心信息不被删减
5. 扩充时可从英文原文中找回已被省略但仍重要的细节
6. 本任务是调整当前中文文本长度，不是重新翻译，不要偏离当前中文文本的核心语义
7. 只输出改写后的中文文本，不要任何解释

改写后的文本：

字数硬约束：输出前请自行按 spoken-char 口径检查一次，只统计中文、英文、数字，不统计标点、空格、换行。最终文本必须落在 171~210 个 spoken chars 之间。如果会低于下限，请保留必要信息和自然口语连接；如果会高于上限，请继续压缩冗余表达。最终只输出改写后的中文文本，不要输出字数、解释或引号。

你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。

任务：对当前文本进行扩充，使其更适合目标配音时长。

当前文本（217字）：
不，这很荒谬，大家都知道这有多荒谬。坦白说，想想民主党在几乎拜登整个任期内的所作所为。有人曾对我说，哦，是你们在针对拜登，不，我们没有。第一个真正针对拜登的是罗伯特·许。这位拜登司法部任命的特别顾问，负责调查他处理机密信息不当的问题。是他说拜登很难被起诉，因为他像个记不清事情、惹人同情的老绅士。正是罗伯特·许提出了这个担忧。然而他们却为奥巴马总统辩护了四年。现在又荒谬地声称要针对特朗普总统。这又是民主党对特朗普无休止的、持续不断的攻击，坦白说，早在2016年起我们就一直看到现在。

英文原文（参考，不要直接翻译）：
No, and it's ridiculous and everyone knows it's ridiculous. And, and frankly, when you think about what Democrats said and did for almost the entire term of President Biden, I mean, I had someone say to me, oh, well, you guys went after President Biden. No, we didn't. The first one to really go after President Biden was Robert Hur. Not until Robert Hur came forward. The special counsel that the Biden Justice Department appointed to look into President Biden's mishandling of classified information. He's the one who said Joe Biden would be difficult to prosecute because he would come across as a sympathetic older gentleman who can't remember things. That was Robert Hur who cited this concern. And yet they defended the President Obama for four years. And now they come some ridiculous claim that they're going to go after President Trump. This is again just Democrats with their endless, constant attack on President Trump, which frankly we have seen since clear back in 2016.

目标字数：约225字（建议控制在202~247字之间）
目标时长区间：尽量落在目标时长的90%~110%
当前需要扩充约0%

要求：
1. 保持原意不变，不要增删核心信息
2. 把隐含意思说得更完整，增加适当口语化过渡语
3. 保持自然口语化，适合视频配音
4. 参考英文原文确保核心信息不被删减
5. 扩充时可从英文原文中找回已被省略但仍重要的细节
6. 本任务是调整当前中文文本长度，不是重新翻译，不要偏离当前中文文本的核心语义
7. 只输出改写后的中文文本，不要任何解释

改写后的文本：

字数硬约束：输出前请自行按 spoken-char 口径检查一次，只统计中文、英文、数字，不统计标点、空格、换行。最终文本必须落在 202~247 个 spoken chars 之间。如果会低于下限，请保留必要信息和自然口语连接；如果会高于上限，请继续压缩冗余表达。最终只输出改写后的中文文本，不要输出字数、解释或引号。

samples.strict_length_retry_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Jim Jordan reacts to Eric Swalwell's resignation | Katie Pavlich Tonight
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KpW50xQGoAU

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
Eric Swalwell → 埃里克·斯瓦尔韦尔
James Boasberg → 詹姆斯·博斯伯格
Kristi Noem → 克里斯蒂·诺姆
DHS → 美国国土安全部
Jamie Raskin → 杰米·拉斯金
Robert Hur → 罗伯特·许
ActBlue → ActBlue
Bryan Steil → 布莱恩·斯泰尔
Dana Remus → 达娜·雷穆斯
Covington → 科文顿律师事务所
NewsNation → NewsNation

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
12. 字数提醒：上一次翻译未达到 min_chars ~ max_chars 的字数要求。如果偏长，请精简表达、删除冗余修饰；如果偏短，请适度补充细节、展开表述。请严格将译文字数控制在 min_chars 到 max_chars 范围内。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

samples.video_title: Warren Buffett officially steps down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway

groups.source_text: All right, we have some other news to tell you about, too. Big changes in Omaha, Nebraska, this week, and the business community at large is taking note. Warren Buffett has officially stepped down as the CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, the company that he built over the last 60 years, and he's handing over the reins to Greg Abel. Buffett remains chairman, of course. That announcement from Buffett about this transition coming back in May at the company's annual meeting, it was a surprise, not only to the shareholders, but to most of the board and to Abel himself. So what changes for Buffett today? Listen to what he told us just a few days after that surprise announcement.

Everything, uh, will be the same. You know, I will come in. Uh, I won't. I won't be up there speaking at the annual meeting, but I'll be in the director section. Maybe you'll interview me at halftime or something of the sort. Who knows? But, uh, Greg will be the decider. He can't imagine how much more he can get accomplished in a week than I can or a month. I mean, just, uh. And at the same time, he's not a distorted individual. Uh, you know, I mean, he likes to play ice hockey with his kids, and he lives what would look like a normal life. And my guess is that if the neighbors didn't know who he was, they wouldn't have any idea that, that, um, on January 1st, he is going to be the decider on a company that employs close to 400,000 people and has got plans around to be around 50 or 100 years from now. And who knows what will happen, but it has a better chance, I think, of being here 100 years from now than any company I can think of.

Abel is inheriting a Berkshire company with a huge stockpile of cash. It was over $381 billion as of the end of September. And after a year when the company gained just over 11% versus the S&P, which was up by almost 17%. Abel, of course, will run all of Berkshire, being the final. Having the final say on both investments and holding companies. Buffett offering some high praise of his successor, listening to that as well.

And Greg's operated more than I have, when you get right down to it. I mean, he's going over to England to run something. He went out. You know, he came to Omaha one time to run a business for a few years. And, uh, he knows there's no secret formula that, uh, only CEOs have or anything of the sort. So I'd rather have Greg handling my money. Than any of the top investment advisors or any of the top CEOs of the United States.
That is a huge endorsement.

reference_translations.source_text: All right, we have some other news to tell you about, too. Big changes in Omaha, Nebraska, this week, and the business community at large is taking note. Warren Buffett has officially stepped down as the CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, the company that he built over the last 60 years, and he's handing over the reins to Greg Abel. Buffett remains chairman, of course. That announcement from Buffett about this transition coming back in May at the company's annual meeting, it was a surprise, not only to the shareholders, but to most of the board and to Abel himself. So what changes for Buffett today? Listen to what he told us just a few days after that surprise announcement.
reference_translations.cn_text: 我们还有些其他消息要告诉大家。本周内布拉斯加州奥马哈市发生了重大变动，整个商业界都在密切关注。沃伦·巴菲特已正式卸任他一手创立了60年的伯克希尔·哈撒韦的首席执行官一职，并将指挥棒交给了格雷格·阿贝尔。当然，巴菲特仍将担任董事长。他在五月份公司年度股东大会上宣布这一交接时，不仅让股东们感到意外，连董事会大多数成员和阿贝尔本人也深感惊讶。那么，巴菲特今天会有什么变化呢？来听听他在那次突发宣布几天后对我们说了些什么。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 接下来关注商业界的一则重磅消息。本周，内布拉斯加州奥马哈市发生巨变：沃伦·巴菲特正式卸任伯克希尔·哈撒韦首席执行官，将掌舵权交给了格雷格·阿贝尔，巴菲特本人则留任董事长。这一决定最早在五月的年度股东大会上宣布，当时不仅股东们感到意外，连董事会成员和阿贝尔本人也深感震惊。那么，巴菲特卸任后会有何变化？来听听他在那次突发宣布几天后，对我们说了些什么。

reference_translations.source_text: Everything, uh, will be the same. You know, I will come in. Uh, I won't. I won't be up there speaking at the annual meeting, but I'll be in the director section. Maybe you'll interview me at halftime or something of the sort. Who knows? But, uh, Greg will be the decider. He can't imagine how much more he can get accomplished in a week than I can or a month. I mean, just, uh. And at the same time, he's not a distorted individual. Uh, you know, I mean, he likes to play ice hockey with his kids, and he lives what would look like a normal life. And my guess is that if the neighbors didn't know who he was, they wouldn't have any idea that, that, um, on January 1st, he is going to be the decider on a company that employs close to 400,000 people and has got plans around to be around 50 or 100 years from now. And who knows what will happen, but it has a better chance, I think, of being here 100 years from now than any company I can think of.
reference_translations.cn_text: 一切都不会变。我会照常过来。虽然我不会再在年度股东大会上发言，但我会坐在董事席上。也许你会在中场休息时采访我之类的。谁知道呢？但格雷格将成为决策者。他无法想象自己在一周或一个月内能完成的工作量比我多出多少。我的意思是，他绝不是一个偏执的人。他喜欢和孩子们打冰球，过着看起来很普通的生活。我猜，如果邻居们不知道他的身份，根本不会想到从1月1日起，他将掌管一家拥有近40万名员工、且规划着未来50到100年发展的公司。未来会怎样谁也说不准，但我认为，比起我所知的任何公司，它更有可能在100年后依然屹立不倒。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 一切都不会变，生活还是会照常进行。我依然会过来，虽然以后我不会再站在年度股东大会的讲台上发言了，但我会坐在董事席位上。也许到了中场休息的时候，你还会过来采访我之类的，谁知道呢？但从今往后，格雷格将成为真正的决策者。他现在可能还无法想象，在接下来的日子里，他每周或每个月能完成的工作量，会比我多出多少。我的意思是，他绝不是一个被权力扭曲的人。他喜欢和孩子们一起打打冰球，过着那种看起来非常普通、平淡的生活。我猜，如果他的邻居们不知道他的真实身份，根本就不会想到，从1月1日起，他将要掌管一家拥有近40万名员工、且规划着未来50到100年发展的庞大公司。未来究竟会怎样，谁也说不准，但我坚信，比起我所知的任何一家公司，它更有可能在100年后依然屹立不倒，继续存在下去。

reference_translations.source_text: Abel is inheriting a Berkshire company with a huge stockpile of cash. It was over $381 billion as of the end of September. And after a year when the company gained just over 11% versus the S&P, which was up by almost 17%. Abel, of course, will run all of Berkshire, being the final. Having the final say on both investments and holding companies. Buffett offering some high praise of his successor, listening to that as well.
reference_translations.cn_text: 阿贝尔接手的是一家拥有巨额现金储备的伯克希尔。截至9月底，现金储备已超过3810亿美元。在过去一年里，公司收益增长了略高于11%，而标普500指数则上涨了近17%。阿贝尔当然会全面执掌伯克希尔，对投资和控股公司拥有最终决定权。巴菲特对他的继任者给予了高度评价，我们也来听听这段话。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 阿贝尔接手的伯克希尔，拥有超3810亿美元的巨额现金储备。过去一年，公司收益增长略超11%，逊于标普500近17%的涨幅。未来，阿贝尔将全面执掌伯克希尔，对投资及控股业务拥有最终决定权。巴菲特对这位继任者给予了高度评价，我们一起来听听。

reference_translations.source_text: And Greg's operated more than I have, when you get right down to it. I mean, he's going over to England to run something. He went out. You know, he came to Omaha one time to run a business for a few years. And, uh, he knows there's no secret formula that, uh, only CEOs have or anything of the sort. So I'd rather have Greg handling my money. Than any of the top investment advisors or any of the top CEOs of the United States.
reference_translations.cn_text: 说实话，格雷格的实操经验比我丰富得多。他曾去英国经营业务，也曾来到奥马哈管理过几年公司。他深知并没有什么只有首席执行官才掌握的秘密公式。所以，比起美国任何顶尖的投资顾问或首席执行官，我更愿意让格雷格来打理我的钱。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 说实话，如果真要论起实操经验，格雷格确实比我丰富得多。他不仅曾远赴英国去经营业务，还亲自来到奥马哈，扎扎实实地管理过好几年的公司。他心里非常清楚，这世上根本没有什么所谓的“首席执行官独家秘籍”或什么神秘公式。正因如此，比起美国那些所谓的顶尖投资顾问，甚至是那些光鲜亮丽的首席执行官们，我反而更愿意把钱交给他来打理，因为我对他有着绝对的信任。

reference_translations.source_text: That is a huge endorsement.
reference_translations.cn_text: 这评价极高。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 这评价极高。

- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=69CppfJO4YQ

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：

Berkshire Hathaway → 伯克希尔·哈撒韦
Greg Abel → 格雷格·阿贝尔
CEO → 首席执行官
S&P → 标普
S&P 500 → 标普500

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：

- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

rewrite_cases.rewrite_prompt:
你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。

任务：对当前文本进行缩短，使其更适合目标配音时长。

当前文本（158字）：
接下来关注商业界的一则重磅消息。本周，内布拉斯加州奥马哈市发生巨变：沃伦·巴菲特正式卸任伯克希尔·哈撒韦首席执行官，将掌舵权交给了格雷格·阿贝尔，巴菲特本人则留任董事长。这一决定最早在五月的年度股东大会上宣布，当时不仅股东们感到意外，连董事会成员和阿贝尔本人也深感震惊。那么，巴菲特卸任后会有何变化？来听听他在那次突发宣布几天后，对我们说了些什么。

英文原文（参考，不要直接翻译）：
All right, we have some other news to tell you about, too. Big changes in Omaha, Nebraska, this week, and the business community at large is taking note. Warren Buffett has officially stepped down as the CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, the company that he built over the last 60 years, and he's handing over the reins to Greg Abel. Buffett remains chairman, of course. That announcement from Buffett about this transition coming back in May at the company's annual meeting, it was a surprise, not only to the shareholders, but to most of the board and to Abel himself. So what changes for Buffett today? Listen to what he told us just a few days after that surprise announcement.

目标字数：约157字（建议控制在141~172字之间）
目标时长区间：尽量落在目标时长的90%~110%
当前需要缩短约0%

要求：
1. 保持原意不变，不要增删核心信息

3. 保持自然口语化，适合视频配音
4. 参考英文原文确保核心信息不被删减
5. 扩充时可从英文原文中找回已被省略但仍重要的细节
6. 本任务是调整当前中文文本长度，不是重新翻译，不要偏离当前中文文本的核心语义
7. 只输出改写后的中文文本，不要任何解释

改写后的文本：

你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。

任务：对当前文本进行扩充，使其更适合目标配音时长。

当前文本（301字）：
一切都不会变，生活还是会照常进行。我依然会过来，虽然以后我不会再站在年度股东大会的讲台上发言了，但我会坐在董事席位上。也许到了中场休息的时候，你还会过来采访我之类的，谁知道呢？但从今往后，格雷格将成为真正的决策者。他现在可能还无法想象，在接下来的日子里，他每周或每个月能完成的工作量，会比我多出多少。我的意思是，他绝不是一个被权力扭曲的人。他喜欢和孩子们一起打打冰球，过着那种看起来非常普通、平淡的生活。我猜，如果他的邻居们不知道他的真实身份，根本就不会想到，从1月1日起，他将要掌管一家拥有近40万名员工、且规划着未来50到100年发展的庞大公司。未来究竟会怎样，谁也说不准，但我坚信，比起我所知的任何一家公司，它更有可能在100年后依然屹立不倒，继续存在下去。

samples.video_title: Jensen Huang – Will Nvidia’s moat persist?

groups.source_text: Right.

And so whether you want to operate it to rent or operate it, if you want to operate to rent, you better have large ecosystem of customers in many industries that be the off takers if you're operating it, if you, if you want to operate it for yourself, um, we, you know, we obviously have the ability to help you operate yourself. Like for example for Elon with xai and um, because we could, we could enable operators, uh, in any company, in any industry, you could use it uh, to build a supercomputer for ah, scientific research. And drug discovery at Lilly. And so we can help them operate their own supercomputer and use it for the entire diversity of drug discovery and biological sciences. Um, that we accelerate M.

And so just a whole bunch of applications that we can address that you can do so with tpusc. Because Nvidia's built CUDA as a fantastic tensor processing unit as well. But it does every life cycle of data processing and computing and AI and so on and so forth. And so our market opportunity is just a lot larger, our reach is a lot greater. And because we have such a large, um, we basically support every application in the world. Now you could build Nvidia systems anywhere and know that there will be customers for it. And so it's a very different thing.
Uh, this is going to be sort of a long question, but you have spectacular revenue. Um, and this revenue is mostly. You're not making 60 billion a quarter from pharma.
Ah.

Now you could build Nvidia systems anywhere and know that there will be customers for it. And so it's a very different thing.

samples.translation_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Jensen Huang – Will Nvidia’s moat persist?
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hrbq66XqtCo

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
GDSII → GDSII 格式
SK Hynix → SK海力士
CoWoS → CoWoS 封装技术
EUV → 极紫外光刻
ASIC → 专用集成电路
MoE → 混合专家模型
vLLM → vLLM 推理框架
DeepSeek → 深度求索
DRAM → 动态随机存取存储器
NVLink → NVLink 高速互联技术
CUDA → CUDA 并行计算平台
TSMC → 台积电
HBM → 高带宽内存

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

samples.video_title: Watch CNBC's full interview with Berkshire Hathaway Chairman Warren Buffett

groups.source_text: It is wonderful to see you this morning.
Fun to be on.

You are on, um, for an interesting reason. For 22 years, you had been holding an annual luncheon, um, an auction for a lunch luncheon to benefit the Glide foundation in San Francisco. You retired from that, from doing that back in 2022 after you'd raised more than $50 million. I think the last auction that you raised, that you did raised $19,000,100 for the Glide Foundation. And you kind of hung it up and said that was going to be the end.

You are back with a new announcement today that there is a new auction that is coming with a twist. This time. It's Warren Buffett, Stephen Curry and Aisha Curry. And they're going to be having a new luncheon to benefit not only the Glide foundation, but the Curry's foundation as well, which is Eat, Learn, Play. And, uh, this auction is going to be held May 7th. It starts at 7:30pm Pacific Time. It closes on May 14th at 7:30pm exactly. And all of the benefits of that is going to go to benefit these two foundations, Glide and the Egg Learn, Play Foundation. How did this come about? Why did you unretire from this?

Well, let me tell you first how I got into it. Uh, because, uh, my. My first wife, Susie, was living in San Francisco, and. And she said to me, this guy is real. And, uh, this guy being Cecil. Yeah, Cecil Williams, who came to that church in 1963. And it was a dying church in a changing part of the neighborhood in San Francisco. And, uh, they weren't glad to see him, uh, the hundred or so parishioners that were left. But he turned it into something that, uh, became.

reference_translations.source_text: It is wonderful to see you this morning.
reference_translations.cn_text: 今早见到您真太好了。

reference_translations.source_text: Fun to be on.
reference_translations.cn_text: 很高兴能来。

reference_translations.source_text: You are on, um, for an interesting reason. For 22 years, you had been holding an annual luncheon, um, an auction for a lunch luncheon to benefit the Glide foundation in San Francisco. You retired from that, from doing that back in 2022 after you'd raised more than $50 million. I think the last auction that you raised, that you did raised $19,000,100 for the Glide Foundation. And you kind of hung it up and said that was going to be the end.
reference_translations.cn_text: 您今天来做客，嗯，是因为一个非常有趣的原因。在过去的22年里，您一直举办年度午餐，嗯，也就是为旧金山格莱德基金会筹款的午餐拍卖活动。您在2022年筹集了超过5000万美元之后，就宣布退休不再办了。我想您最后一次举办的拍卖，为格莱德基金会筹集了1900万零100美元。然后您就算是金盆洗手了，说那将是最后一次。

reference_translations.source_text: You are back with a new announcement today that there is a new auction that is coming with a twist. This time. It's Warren Buffett, Stephen Curry and Aisha Curry. And they're going to be having a new luncheon to benefit not only the Glide foundation, but the Curry's foundation as well, which is Eat, Learn, Play. And, uh, this auction is going to be held May 7th. It starts at 7:30pm Pacific Time. It closes on May 14th at 7:30pm exactly. And all of the benefits of that is going to go to benefit these two foundations, Glide and the Egg Learn, Play Foundation. How did this come about? Why did you unretire from this?
reference_translations.cn_text: 今天您带来个新消息，即将有一场带点新花样的全新拍卖。这次是沃伦·巴菲特、斯蒂芬·库里和阿耶莎·库里。他们将举办一场新午餐会，不仅资助格莱德基金会，还资助库里夫妇的 Eat, Learn, Play 基金会。拍卖将于5月7日太平洋时间晚7点半开始，到5月14日晚7点半准时结束。所有收益都将用于资助这两个基金会。这是怎么促成的？您为何决定复出呢？

reference_translations.source_text: Well, let me tell you first how I got into it. Uh, because, uh, my. My first wife, Susie, was living in San Francisco, and. And she said to me, this guy is real. And, uh, this guy being Cecil. Yeah, Cecil Williams, who came to that church in 1963. And it was a dying church in a changing part of the neighborhood in San Francisco. And, uh, they weren't glad to see him, uh, the hundred or so parishioners that were left. But he turned it into something that, uh, became.
reference_translations.cn_text: 嗯，让我先告诉你，我是怎么参与进这件事情里来的。呃，其实是因为，呃，我的……我的第一任妻子苏茜，她当时住在旧金山，然后，然后她对我说，这个人真的很了不起。而且，呃，这个人就是塞西尔。对，就是塞西尔·威廉姆斯，他是在1963年的时候来到了那家教堂。当时那是一个在旧金山不断变化的街区里，一家快要倒闭的教堂。而且，呃，那里的人并不怎么欢迎他的到来，呃，也就是当时剩下的那一百来个教区居民。但是，他却把它变成了一个，呃，最终让它成为了一个……

samples.translation_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

- 标题：Watch CNBC's full interview with Berkshire Hathaway Chairman Warren Buffett
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-6ladc96Sk8

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
Berkshire Hathaway → 伯克希尔·哈撒韦

Warren Buffett → 沃伦·巴菲特
Stephen Curry → 斯蒂芬·库里
Ayesha Curry → 阿耶莎·库里

Jay Powell → 杰罗姆·鲍威尔

Bill Gates → 比尔·盖茨
Melinda Gates → 梅琳达·盖茨
Jeffrey Epstein → 杰弗里·爱泼斯坦

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

rewrite_cases.rewrite_prompt:
你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。

任务：对当前文本进行缩短，使其更适合目标配音时长。

当前文本（5字）：
很高兴能来。

英文原文（参考，不要直接翻译）：
Fun to be on.

目标字数：约2字（建议控制在1~2字之间）
目标时长区间：尽量落在目标时长的90%~110%
当前需要缩短约112%

要求：
1. 保持原意不变，不要增删核心信息
2. 删减冗余词汇、连接词，精简表达
3. 保持自然口语化，适合视频配音
4. 参考英文原文确保核心信息不被删减
5. 扩充时可从英文原文中找回已被省略但仍重要的细节
6. 本任务是调整当前中文文本长度，不是重新翻译，不要偏离当前中文文本的核心语义
7. 只输出改写后的中文文本，不要任何解释

改写后的文本：

字数硬约束：输出前请自行按 spoken-char 口径检查一次，只统计中文、英文、数字，不统计标点、空格、换行。最终文本必须落在 1~2 个 spoken chars 之间。如果会低于下限，请保留必要信息和自然口语连接；如果会高于上限，请继续压缩冗余表达。最终只输出改写后的中文文本，不要输出字数、解释或引号。

你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。

任务：对当前文本进行扩充，使其更适合目标配音时长。

当前文本（151字）：
今天您带来个新消息，即将有一场带点新花样的全新拍卖。这次是沃伦·巴菲特、斯蒂芬·库里和阿耶莎·库里。他们将举办一场新午餐会，不仅资助格莱德基金会，还资助库里夫妇的 Eat, Learn, Play 基金会。拍卖将于5月7日太平洋时间晚7点半开始，到5月14日晚7点半准时结束。所有收益都将用于资助这两个基金会。这是怎么促成的？您为何决定复出呢？

英文原文（参考，不要直接翻译）：
You are back with a new announcement today that there is a new auction that is coming with a twist. This time. It's Warren Buffett, Stephen Curry and Aisha Curry. And they're going to be having a new luncheon to benefit not only the Glide foundation, but the Curry's foundation as well, which is Eat, Learn, Play. And, uh, this auction is going to be held May 7th. It starts at 7:30pm Pacific Time. It closes on May 14th at 7:30pm exactly. And all of the benefits of that is going to go to benefit these two foundations, Glide and the Egg Learn, Play Foundation. How did this come about? Why did you unretire from this?

目标字数：约159字（建议控制在143~174字之间）
目标时长区间：尽量落在目标时长的90%~110%
当前需要扩充约0%

要求：
1. 保持原意不变，不要增删核心信息
2. 把隐含意思说得更完整，增加适当口语化过渡语
3. 保持自然口语化，适合视频配音
4. 参考英文原文确保核心信息不被删减
5. 扩充时可从英文原文中找回已被省略但仍重要的细节
6. 本任务是调整当前中文文本长度，不是重新翻译，不要偏离当前中文文本的核心语义
7. 只输出改写后的中文文本，不要任何解释

改写后的文本：

字数硬约束：输出前请自行按 spoken-char 口径检查一次，只统计中文、英文、数字，不统计标点、空格、换行。最终文本必须落在 143~174 个 spoken chars 之间。如果会低于下限，请保留必要信息和自然口语连接；如果会高于上限，请继续压缩冗余表达。最终只输出改写后的中文文本，不要输出字数、解释或引号。

samples.strict_length_retry_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Watch CNBC's full interview with Berkshire Hathaway Chairman Warren Buffett
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-6ladc96Sk8

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
Berkshire Hathaway → 伯克希尔·哈撒韦
Glide Foundation → 格莱德基金会
Eat, Learn, Play Foundation → Eat, Learn, Play 基金会
Warren Buffett → 沃伦·巴菲特
Stephen Curry → 斯蒂芬·库里
Ayesha Curry → 阿耶莎·库里
Cecil Williams → 塞西尔·威廉姆斯
Greg Abel → 格雷格·艾贝尔
Charlie Munger → 查理·芒格
Salomon Brothers → 所罗门兄弟
Jay Powell → 杰罗姆·鲍威尔
Strategic Air Command (SAC) → 战略空军司令部
Bill Gates → 比尔·盖茨
Melinda Gates → 梅琳达·盖茨
Jeffrey Epstein → 杰弗里·爱泼斯坦
Giving Pledge → 捐赠誓言

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
12. 字数提醒：上一次翻译未达到 min_chars ~ max_chars 的字数要求。如果偏长，请精简表达、删除冗余修饰；如果偏短，请适度补充细节、展开表述。请严格将译文字数控制在 min_chars 到 max_chars 范围内。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

samples.video_title: Warren Buffett says he hasn’t spoken to Bill Gates since ‘whole thing’ with Epstein files

groups.source_text: Let's talk about the latest when it comes to the Epstein files, because today you've got Warren Buffett in a new interview with CNBC saying he hasn't spoken to Bill Gates since what he calls the whole thing with the Epstein documents that went down. Watch this.

I don't want to be in a position where I know things at the moment. I could get called as a witness, but I think until it gets cleared up, I don't, I just don't think it makes sense to do a lot of talking.

These, ah, are Warren Buffett's first public comments about Gates since these files came out. Remember, he's the chairman of Berkshire Hathaway. He donated billions of dollars to the Gates Foundation. But he says he doesn't regret any of that. Gates and Epstein became friends in 2011, three years after Epstein pleaded guilty to soliciting a minor for prostitution in 2008. Last month, remember we told you this. Gates told staffers at a town hall it was a huge mistake to have spent time with Epstein, according to reporting by the Wall Street Journal, and said he didn't do or see anything illicit. Let me bring in Ryan Reilly, who's following this story for us. Um, Buffett is one of the richest people in the world. And he said he was shocked at how Epstein was able to win over and basically con, in his words, everybody. And that has real consequences. Right. I mean, the concern from, from Buffett, um, he really laid it out there.

Yeah, exactly. I mean, you know, this has had these huge repercussions across these circles, the sort of circles that Warren Buffett typically deals in. And here's a little bit more of what he had to say in the interview.
He had a way of conning everybody. Men are going to like sex and, uh, some, some of them are going to like not paying taxes and whatever it was. He figured out what their weakness was.

Let me bring in Ryan Reilly, who's following this story for us. Um, Buffett is one of the richest people in the world. And he said he was shocked at how Epstein was able to win over and basically con, in his words, everybody. And that has real consequences. Right. I mean, the concern from, from Buffett, um, he really laid it out there.
reference_translations.cn_text: 这是文件曝光后，巴菲特首次公开评论盖茨。他是伯克希尔·哈撒韦董事长，曾向盖茨基金会捐赠数十亿美元，但他表示并不后悔。盖茨与爱泼斯坦在2011年成为朋友，那是在爱泼斯坦2008年承认教唆未成年人卖淫罪的三年之后。上个月据《华尔街日报》报道，盖茨在内部会议上告诉员工，与爱泼斯坦交往是个巨大错误，并称自己未做也未见过任何非法之事。现在我们来连线一直跟进此事的瑞安·赖利。巴菲特作为全球最富有的人之一，表示他对爱泼斯坦竟能赢得并“骗过”所有人感到震惊，而这带来了实实在在的后果。是的，巴菲特已经把担忧说得很明白了。

reference_translations.source_text: Yeah, exactly. I mean, you know, this has had these huge repercussions across these circles, the sort of circles that Warren Buffett typically deals in. And here's a little bit more of what he had to say in the interview.

reference_translations.cn_text: 他这个人啊，有一套自己的办法，能把所有人都给骗过去。你想啊，男人嘛，大多都喜欢性那方面的事，呃，还有些人呢，就喜欢逃税啊，或者搞点别的什么名堂。他总能琢磨透，找到他们每个人的弱点。

samples.strict_length_retry_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Warren Buffett says he hasn’t spoken to Bill Gates since ‘whole thing’ with Epstein files
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YNpOjymKsZc

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
Epstein files → 爱泼斯坦文件
Warren Buffett → 沃伦·巴菲特
Bill Gates → 比尔·盖茨
Berkshire Hathaway → 伯克希尔·哈撒韦
Gates Foundation → 盖茨基金会
CNBC → CNBC
NBC News → NBC新闻
Pam Bondi → 帕姆·邦迪

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
12. 字数提醒：上一次翻译未达到 min_chars ~ max_chars 的字数要求。如果偏长，请精简表达、删除冗余修饰；如果偏短，请适度补充细节、展开表述。请严格将译文字数控制在 min_chars 到 max_chars 范围内。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

samples.video_title: Retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral Tim Gallaudet on Grusch, UAP claims: Full Interview | Reality Check

glossary.National Oceanic Atmospheric Administrat: 美国国家海洋和大气管理局
glossary.NOAA: 美国国家海洋和大气管理局
glossary.UAP: 不明异常现象 (Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena)
glossary.NGA: 美国国家地理空间情报局
glossary.Americans for Safe Aerospace: 美国航空航天安全协会

groups.source_text: A few, a few reasons for that, Ross. First off, I had a job where I was the superintendent of the U.S. naval Observatory and I had a team of astrophysicists that uh, monitored the stars and made star catalogs. And I learned a lot about astrophysics then and I came to appreciate how large the universe is. And you just cannot conclude when you know that there's 200 to 300 billion stars in our galaxy and there's hundreds of billions of galaxies. To think that we're alone is incredibly arrogant for one. And then two, as I mentioned, I saw on a uh, secret email the Go fast video in 2015 I believe. And then I knew that was not our technology.
Are you disappointed that the US government's not doing more data driven empirical research investigating UAPs?

Uh, I am. I think it's time now and with the Sol foundation coming uh, out in its inaugural symposium here, this is exactly what we're trying to do is make policy recommendations to the US Government to begin more systematic scientific study of UAP in a number of ways. And what Sol is looking at is both hard science trying to acquire some of the materials we have from the crash retrieval program so we can learn more about that technology which could have incredible benefits to society, as well as some of the humanities aspects. So things like sociology and anthropology, uh, and some of the uh, I guess ethics issues possibly.

The problem, Tim, is that a lot of the government is still in absolute denial. I mean you talk as a fact about a crash retrieval program and we're talking here about retrieved non human technology, aren't we?
Yes.

reference_translations.source_text: A few, a few reasons for that, Ross. First off, I had a job where I was the superintendent of the U.S. naval Observatory and I had a team of astrophysicists that uh, monitored the stars and made star catalogs. And I learned a lot about astrophysics then and I came to appreciate how large the universe is. And you just cannot conclude when you know that there's 200 to 300 billion stars in our galaxy and there's hundreds of billions of galaxies. To think that we're alone is incredibly arrogant for one. And then two, as I mentioned, I saw on a uh, secret email the Go fast video in 2015 I believe. And then I knew that was not our technology.
reference_translations.cn_text: 有几个原因，罗斯。首先，我曾担任美国海军天文台的负责人，我手下有一支天体物理学家团队，呃，负责监测恒星并制作星表。那时我学到了很多天体物理知识，并开始认识到宇宙有多么浩瀚。当你了解到我们银河系有2000亿到3000亿颗恒星，而宇宙中有数千亿个星系时，你根本无法得出我们孤独存在的结论。首先，认为我们独一无二是极其傲慢的。其次，正如我提到的，我在一封，呃，机密邮件中看到了‘Go Fast’视频，我记得是在2015年。那时我就知道，那不是我们的技术。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 有几个原因，罗斯。首先，我曾担任美国海军天文台的负责人，我手下有一支天体物理学家团队，呃，负责监测恒星并制作星表。那时我学到了很多天体物理知识，并开始认识到宇宙有多么浩瀚。当你了解到我们银河系有2000亿到3000亿颗恒星，而宇宙中有数千亿个星系时，你根本无法得出我们孤独存在的结论。首先，认为我们独一无二是极其傲慢的。其次，正如我提到的，我在一封，呃，机密邮件中看到了‘Go Fast’视频，我记得是在2015年。那时我就知道，那不是我们的技术。

reference_translations.source_text: Are you disappointed that the US government's not doing more data driven empirical research investigating UAPs?
reference_translations.cn_text: 你是否对美国政府在调查不明异常现象方面，没有进行更多数据驱动的实证研究感到失望？
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 你是否对美国政府在调查不明异常现象方面，没有进行更多数据驱动的实证研究感到失望？

reference_translations.source_text: Uh, I am. I think it's time now and with the Sol foundation coming uh, out in its inaugural symposium here, this is exactly what we're trying to do is make policy recommendations to the US Government to begin more systematic scientific study of UAP in a number of ways. And what Sol is looking at is both hard science trying to acquire some of the materials we have from the crash retrieval program so we can learn more about that technology which could have incredible benefits to society, as well as some of the humanities aspects. So things like sociology and anthropology, uh, and some of the uh, I guess ethics issues possibly.
reference_translations.cn_text: 呃，是的。我认为现在是时候了。随着索尔基金会即将举行首届研讨会，这正是我们试图做的事情：向美国政府提出政策建议，以多种方式开始对不明异常现象进行更系统的科学研究。索尔基金会关注的是硬科学，试图获取我们从坠毁回收项目中得到的一些材料，以便我们能更多地了解那项技术，这可能对社会带来难以置信的好处；同时也关注一些人文学科方面，比如社会学、人类学，呃，以及一些，我猜可能还有伦理问题。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 呃，是的。我认为现在是时候了。随着索尔基金会即将举行首届研讨会，这正是我们试图做的事情：向美国政府提出政策建议，以多种方式开始对不明异常现象进行更系统的科学研究。索尔基金会关注的是硬科学，试图获取我们从坠毁回收项目中得到的一些材料，以便我们能更多地了解那项技术，这可能对社会带来难以置信的好处；同时也关注一些人文学科方面，比如社会学、人类学，呃，以及一些，我猜可能还有伦理问题。

reference_translations.source_text: The problem, Tim, is that a lot of the government is still in absolute denial. I mean you talk as a fact about a crash retrieval program and we're talking here about retrieved non human technology, aren't we?
reference_translations.cn_text: 问题是，蒂姆，政府中很多人仍然完全否认。我的意思是，你谈论坠毁回收项目时把它当作事实，而我们在这里谈论的是回收的非人类技术，对吧？
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 问题是，蒂姆，政府中很多人仍然完全否认。我的意思是，你谈论坠毁回收项目时把它当作事实，而我们在这里谈论的是回收的非人类技术，对吧？

reference_translations.source_text: Yes.
reference_translations.cn_text: 是的。
reference_translations.tts_cn_text: 是的。

samples.translation_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Retired U.S. Navy Rear Admiral Tim Gallaudet on Grusch, UAP claims: Full Interview | Reality Check
- 来源：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Qh_h5OUMKIE

术语表（请严格遵循以下翻译）：
NewsNation → 新闻国家
Tim Gallaudet → 蒂姆·加劳德特
National Oceanic Atmospheric Administration → 美国国家海洋和大气管理局

NASA → 美国国家航空航天局
David Grusch → 大卫·格鲁施
Schumer amendment → 舒默修正案
Chuck Schumer → 查克·舒默
Ryan Graves → 瑞安·格雷夫斯
David Fravor → 大卫·弗雷弗
NGA → 美国国家地理空间情报局
Americans for Safe Aerospace → 美国航空航天安全协会
Go fast video → “Go Fast”视频
U.S. Naval Observatory → 美国海军天文台
Sol Foundation → 索尔基金会
Sean Kirkpatrick → 肖恩·柯克帕特里克
Alex Dietrich → 亚历克斯·迪特里希
Chris Mellon → 克里斯·梅隆
Politico → 政治报

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

输入（JSON数组）：
[
  {
    "segment_id": 27,
    "speaker_id": "speaker_c",
    "target_duration_seconds": 41.2,
    "source_text": "A few, a few reasons for that, Ross. First off, I had a job where I was the superintendent of the U.S. naval Observatory and I had a team of astrophysicists that uh, monitored the stars and made star catalogs. And I learned a lot about astrophysics then and I came to appreciate how large the universe is. And you just cannot conclude when you know that there's 200 to 300 billion stars in our galaxy and there's hundreds of billions of galaxies. To think that we're alone is incredibly arrogant for one. And then two, as I mentioned, I saw on a uh, secret email the Go fast video in 2015 I believe. And then I knew that was not our technology.",
    "source_word_count": 124,
    "source_words_per_second": 3.008,
    "target_chars": 223,
    "min_chars": 189,
    "max_chars": 256,
    "target_chars_hint": 223,
    "voice_chars_per_second": 4.5
  },
  {
    "segment_id": 28,
    "speaker_id": "speaker_b",
    "target_duration_seconds": 7.0,
    "source_text": "Are you disappointed that the US government's not doing more data driven empirical research investigating UAPs?",
    "source_word_count": 16,

I think it's time now and with the Sol foundation coming uh, out in its inaugural symposium here, this is exactly what we're trying to do is make policy recommendations to the US Government to begin more systematic scientific study of UAP in a number of ways. And what Sol is looking at is both hard science trying to acquire some of the materials we have from the crash retrieval program so we can learn more about that technology which could have incredible benefits to society, as well as some of the humanities aspects. So things like sociology and anthropology, uh, and some of the uh, I guess ethics issues possibly.",

    "target_duration_seconds": 11.1,
    "source_text": "The problem, Tim, is that a lot of the government is still in absolute denial. I mean you talk as a fact about a crash retrieval program and we're talking here about retrieved non human technology, aren't we?",

    "source_words_per_second": 3.411,

samples.video_title: Extended interview: Jamie Dimon on AI, Iran and more

groups.source_text: Let's talk about affordable, uh, housing. One of the key parts of this initiative of yours. There's a plan right now in Congress to potentially prevent large investors from buying up single family home. Do you support a ban on that kind of investment?

I hate to tell this, it's basically irrelevant. It's not. It's only, I think they're doing it, 350 homes, only 1% of the homes owned by what you would call large companies. So I don't think it's going to change any calculus. I'm not sure it's great because some people want to rent their homes or not and these. So they go in, they put in money. I probably wouldn't do it, but if it gets done, it's okay. If you want to fix affordable housing, you can change rules and regulators around mortgages that would reduce the cost of mortgage by 30 or 40 basis points and not create any additional risk, in my view. And take affordable housing laws, mostly local, sometimes state, and you know, don't lard them with job programs; let people build them. You know, if you read the list of ridiculous rules, why it can't get done, you can build a lot more starter homes and starter apartments that people could buy a lot cheaper all over the country. And there are some people doing it. Well, I can send you the list of what cities have got it together and they're building stuff. And like in California, I think I read it's 50% more to build, quote, an affordable home than it is in Nevada because those rules and regulations. So there's a lot of things to do to fix it. That's what we should be doing. That'll make a much bigger difference than limiting what, you know, big, uh, private equity guys can do.

Let's talk about, uh, AI, you've talked a lot about it in recent months. You've admitted that it could reduce jobs and it could reduce jobs across the economy somewhat quickly. Too quickly. Uh, how concerned are you really about the potential for, as you put it, social unrest, even revolution?

Yeah. So this is the way I put it, uh, in the context for all of us. I believe that 30 years from now, a lot of people, your kids are probably working three and a half days a week. They'll probably live to 100. And a lot of the diseases afflicted a lot of us, they won't get. There'll be cures to cancer. Cars will be safer, planes will be safer, Life will be better. You can hike more, do all the things you're talking about.
The Jetsons from the 1960s. Walter Cronkite anchored a special about how the future that we're living in right now is supposed to be what you're saying.

samples.translation_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：Extended interview: Jamie Dimon on AI, Iran and more

Aviation High School → 航空高中

Strait of Hormuz → 霍尔木兹海峡
Qarg Island → 卡尔格岛
Ukraine → 乌克兰
Russia → 俄罗斯
Iran → 伊朗
North Korea → 朝鲜
China → 中国
Baltimore → 巴尔的摩
Detroit → 底特律
Covid → 新冠疫情
Hollywood Squares → 好莱坞方块
Queens → 皇后区
Walter Cronkite → 沃尔特·克朗凯特
The Jetsons → 杰森一家
PE growth → 市盈率增长
EQ → 情商

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

samples.video_title: How Pakistan, China Played Roles in US-Iran Ceasefire

groups.source_text: And then you talked about China. Some say perhaps China was the real power broker here, that it was China that actually sealed the deal, so to speak.

Yeah, um, there is reporting from the New York Times and others that there was a last-minute intervention by China to get involved. We know that Pakistan and China had a joint peace proposal last week. Of course, China is the biggest buyer of Iranian oil. They would presumably be involved in helping to finance any reconstruction after the war. And they're just a key economic partner for Iran altogether. So, we're still trying to report that out. What kind of exactly went down behind the scenes and what pressure China put on.
What role, if any, might China play in this?

Well, China's been propping up the Iranian economy now since the JCPOA, since the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action. They put $400 billion into Iran's $400 billion economy and have not seen the return on investment. And if you look at the amount of oil that China depends on from Iran, they do have a say. Without China, without Russia, Iran is really left friendless in a way. And, you know, the way they've treated their de facto allies, Qatar, and their de facto allies, Oman, during this conflict has further isolated the regime.

So China played a huge role. And this is in China's interest. It's also in China's interest and Russia's that Iran does not leave the Non-Proliferation Treaty. And that's something that they threatened to do in this conflict, and they've walked away from that. And I do believe that's because of China and Russia, Russia's pressure on the regime.

reference_translations.cn_text: 你刚才提到了中国。有人认为中国才是真正的幕后推手，是北京方面最终敲定了这项协议。

What kind of exactly went down behind the scenes and what pressure China put on.

samples.translation_prompt:
你是专业的视频配音翻译专家。任务是把英文视频转录稿翻译成自然流畅的中文口播文本。

视频信息：
- 标题：How Pakistan, China Played Roles in US-Iran Ceasefire

Islamabad → 伊斯兰堡

Truth Social → Truth Social
China → 中国
Iran → 伊朗
US → 美国
Eastern Time → 美国东部时间
Straits of Hormuz → 霍尔木兹海峡
Iran Armed Forces → 伊朗武装部队
Gulf regions → 海湾地区
Biden administration → 拜登政府
India → 印度
Asim Munir → 阿西姆·穆尼尔
White House → 白宫
Trump → 特朗普
Saudi Arabia → 沙特阿拉伯
New York Times → 纽约时报
JCPOA → 伊朗核协议
Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action → 联合全面行动计划
Non-Proliferation Treaty → 核不扩散条约
Qatar → 卡塔尔
Oman → 阿曼

这些翻译将直接用于中文 TTS 配音，核心目标是让中文配音时长与原英文段落时长大致一致。请特别注意：
1. 每段都标注了 target_duration_seconds（原文段落时长），翻译时请自然地控制中文长度，使配音时长接近该目标。
2. 不要机械地按字数公式凑字，而是根据原文的语速节奏、信息密度来判断中文应该翻多长。
3. 宁可适度意译、精简表达，也不要逐字直译导致配音明显超时。
4. 如果原文信息密度高，可用更紧凑的中文表达方式保留核心信息。
5. 翻译结果将用于配音，不要写成书面字幕腔，要适合人声朗读。
6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

12. 字数提醒：上一次翻译未达到 min_chars ~ max_chars 的字数要求。如果偏长，请精简表达、删除冗余修饰；如果偏短，请适度补充细节、展开表述。请严格将译文字数控制在 min_chars 到 max_chars 范围内。

   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"

9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

samples.video_title: Charlie Munger’s Life Advice: Let Go of Things That Don’t Matter | Final Interview with CNBC 2023

groups.source_text: I think another thing you've said is whatever you are, um, wealth and age will make you more so.
Yeah, sure.
You've given away a lot of your money.

Yeah, but not like Warren. More than half the Munger money has already been passed to the descendants. So I made exactly the opposite. It was a smaller amount of money, but I made exactly the opposite decision for the majority of our money. The majority of his money he gave away. Now he didn't exactly give it away. It goes to public foundations to go on for another hundred years. That's not exactly giving it. It's not exactly like wasting it. It's having something significant.
Is there anything left on your bucket list? Anything you'd like to do?

reference_translations.source_text: I think another thing you've said is whatever you are, um, wealth and age will make you more so.
reference_translations.cn_text: 我想您还说过另一句话，无论你本性如何，呃，财富和年龄都会让你变本加厉。

reference_translations.source_text: Yeah, sure.
reference_translations.cn_text: 对，没错。

reference_translations.source_text: You've given away a lot of your money.
reference_translations.cn_text: 您已经捐出了很大一部分钱。

reference_translations.source_text: Yeah, but not like Warren. More than half the Munger money has already been passed to the descendants. So I made exactly the opposite. It was a smaller amount of money, but I made exactly the opposite decision for the majority of our money. The majority of his money he gave away. Now he didn't exactly give it away. It goes to public foundations to go on for another hundred years. That's not exactly giving it. It's not exactly like wasting it. It's having something significant.
reference_translations.cn_text: 是的，但没像沃伦那么多。芒格家族一半以上的钱都已经传给了后代。所以我做了完全相反的决定。虽然钱没他那么多，但我对我们大部分钱做出了完全相反的决定。他把大部分钱都捐了。不过也不能说是完全捐掉。这些钱流向了公共基金会，还能再运作个一百年。这不完全是捐赠。也不完全是浪费。这是在做一些有意义的事情。

reference_translations.source_text: Is there anything left on your bucket list? Anything you'd like to do?
reference_translations.cn_text: 您的遗愿清单上还有什么吗？还有什么想做的吗？

6. 所有人物姓名必须优先使用中文常见译名，不要保留英文人名。
   例如：Elon Musk -> 埃隆·马斯克，Sam Altman -> 萨姆·奥特曼，Naval Ravikant -> 纳瓦尔·拉维坎特。
7. 公司、产品、品牌、模型名称若已有常见中文译法，优先使用中文；若没有稳定中文译法，可保留原文。
9. 这是双人访谈，请区分两个说话人的语气、措辞和交流关系。
12. 字数提醒：上一次翻译未达到 min_chars ~ max_chars 的字数要求。如果偏长，请精简表达、删除冗余修饰；如果偏短，请适度补充细节、展开表述。请严格将译文字数控制在 min_chars 到 max_chars 范围内。
8. **保留口语化节奏（重要，影响 TTS 时长匹配）**：当原文有较多口头禅、语气词、思考停顿、重复表达时（典型如 "well..." / "you know" / "I mean" / "I would say" / "uh, um" / "kind of" / "sort of" / "you see" / 同义反复），中文译文应**适度保留**这些口语化元素，对应翻译为：
   - 思考类 → "嗯"、"呃"、"我想说"、"这个嘛"
   - 缓冲类 → "你知道的"、"我是说"、"怎么讲呢"、"应该说"
   - 强调类 → "可以说"、"应该这么说"、"我是这么觉得的"
   - 同义反复 → 适当保留"换句话说……"或者用近义词复述

   **判断原则**：当 source_words_per_second 偏低（< 2.0 词/秒）或 target_duration_seconds 较长但原文信息量不大时，说明原说话人节奏慢、用了大量口语缓冲。这种情况下精简翻译会让 TTS 时长**远短于**目标，反而需要事后强制拉伸，破坏听感。**宁可让中文带点"啰嗦感"贴近原节奏，也不要为了"流畅"过度精简。**
9. 每个 segment 独立翻译，但要保持上下文连贯。
10. 只输出 JSON，不要任何其他文字。

每个 segment 都提供了以下元数据（按约束强度排序）：

**硬约束**（请严格遵守，超出会导致译文与原文长度严重失配）：
- target_duration_seconds：原文段落时长（秒）
- target_chars：本段建议的中文字数 = 原文英文词数 × 1.8（中英自然翻译比）。这是「保留原文信息密度的自然中文长度」。
- min_chars ~ max_chars：target_chars 的 ±15% 浮动区间。译文字数请落在此区间内。

**参考信息**（帮助你判断详略策略，不直接约束字数）：
- source_words_per_second：原说话人的英文语速（词/秒）。值大表示信息密集；值小表示节奏宽松、口语词多。
- voice_chars_per_second：选定中文音色的合成语速（字/秒）。**仅供参考**：当 voice_chars_per_second × target_duration_seconds 与 target_chars 接近时，TTS 合成时长会自然贴近目标；差距大时由后续阶段调整音色 speed 或拉伸对齐，无需你为此修改字数。
- target_chars_hint：与 target_chars 同值（保留以兼容旧 prompt 自定义）。

如何使用这些字段：
- 原文信息密度高（source_words_per_second 偏高、或内容含大量数字/术语/专有名词）：译文字数可接近 max_chars，优先保留信息。
- 原文节奏宽松（source_words_per_second 偏低、口语化连词多）：译文字数可接近 min_chars，但**仍要保留所有口头禅和语气词**（参见上面第 8 条），让译文自然铺满 target_duration。
- 信息密度正常：按 target_chars 落笔，落在 min/max 区间内即可。

输入（JSON数组）：

samples.video_title: The One-Person Business Model (How To Productize Yourself)

groups.source_text: And then the last part of the motto is enjoy life, and that is usually a byproduct of both— of working less, resting more, earning more, and just having the time and location freedom to be able to do what you choose to do with your life. That's what I'm all about. And it's so weird when I see people in the comments or other things like challenging this notion. It's like, dude, like, do you just believe that you have no fucking control over your life? Like, yeah, you're not gonna have it right now. It takes some time.

I could go on about this all day, but like, dude, like, just believe in yourself, as simple as that sounds, just like, work towards a better future for yourself. But the thing is, I also fell into the trap of wanting to live the life of other people. Beachfront views and the sand in my toes and a tan like David Hasselhoff and sipping coconuts on the beach while working from my laptop. And that was until I experienced that life.

I'm not here to kind of shit on the digital nomad culture. I think it's great. I really do think it's great. But like, who wants to do that? It kind of— it gets old very quick where you're trying to work on the beach and you can't even see your screen because the glare from the sun, and it gets too hot and you have to reapply sunscreen, and you're like, I'm fucking thirsty, I'd rather just be inside working. And so I don't know, that kind of stuff, it's like, yeah, you have to go through trial and error to understand what you want.

You have to figure out what you don't want in order to figure out what you want. But you have to understand that the, the glamour and the different lifestyles that people promote, it's for— it's dopamine, right? You're, you're seeing something that you want, and then once you achieve it, if you don't appreciate it and make it a part of your life, then that is going to fade very quickly. The Wi-Fi connection in those areas gets old. The partying and the language barrier and doing the things that you see on social media as a highlight reel, they really do get old.

And so the purpose of this video, I want to talk about Moderation and sustainability, because we just talked about how some of those things, or at least the glamorous things, are not truly sustainable. So I want to present a business model that allows you to create your ideal lifestyle through practice, experimentation, and iteration.

reference_translations.source_text: And then the last part of the motto is enjoy life, and that is usually a byproduct of both— of working less, resting more, earning more, and just having the time and location freedom to be able to do what you choose to do with your life. That's what I'm all about. And it's so weird when I see people in the comments or other things like challenging this notion. It's like, dude, like, do you just believe that you have no fucking control over your life? Like, yeah, you're not gonna have it right now. It takes some time.
reference_translations.cn_text: 然后座右铭的最后一部分是享受生活，这通常是前两者的副产品——少工作、多休息、多赚钱，并且拥有时间和地点的自由，能够用你的生命去做你选择做的事情。这就是我的追求。当我在评论区或其他地方看到人们质疑这个观念时，我觉得很奇怪。就像，老兄，难道你真的认为你对自己的生活完全没有掌控力吗？我是说，对，你现在可能还做不到。这需要一些时间。

reference_translations.source_text: I could go on about this all day, but like, dude, like, just believe in yourself, as simple as that sounds, just like, work towards a better future for yourself. But the thing is, I also fell into the trap of wanting to live the life of other people. Beachfront views and the sand in my toes and a tan like David Hasselhoff and sipping coconuts on the beach while working from my laptop. And that was until I experienced that life.
reference_translations.cn_text: 关于这个我可以说上一整天，但是，老兄，就像，相信你自己，听起来很简单，就是为自己更好的未来而努力。但问题是，我也曾陷入过想要过别人那种生活的陷阱。海滨美景，脚趾间的沙子，晒出像大卫·哈塞尔霍夫那样的古铜色皮肤，在海滩上一边喝着椰汁一边用笔记本电脑工作。直到我真正体验了那种生活。

reference_translations.source_text: I'm not here to kind of shit on the digital nomad culture. I think it's great. I really do think it's great. But like, who wants to do that? It kind of— it gets old very quick where you're trying to work on the beach and you can't even see your screen because the glare from the sun, and it gets too hot and you have to reapply sunscreen, and you're like, I'm fucking thirsty, I'd rather just be inside working. And so I don't know, that kind of stuff, it's like, yeah, you have to go through trial and error to understand what you want.
reference_translations.cn_text: 我不是在贬低数字游民文化。我真的觉得它很棒。但谁想一直那样呢？新鲜感很快就会消失，当你想在海滩上工作，阳光却刺眼得看不清屏幕，天太热还得重涂防晒霜，你会觉得渴死了，还不如待在室内工作。所以怎么说呢，你必须通过不断的试错，才能明白自己到底想要什么。

reference_translations.source_text: You have to figure out what you don't want in order to figure out what you want. But you have to understand that the, the glamour and the different lifestyles that people promote, it's for— it's dopamine, right? You're, you're seeing something that you want, and then once you achieve it, if you don't appreciate it and make it a part of your life, then that is going to fade very quickly. The Wi-Fi connection in those areas gets old. The partying and the language barrier and doing the things that you see on social media as a highlight reel, they really do get old.
reference_translations.cn_text: 你必须先弄清自己不想要什么，才能知道想要什么。但你要明白，人们推崇的光鲜生活，其实就是多巴胺。看到想要的东西，一旦得到，若不懂得欣赏并融入生活，这种感觉很快就会消退。那些地方的Wi-Fi会让人厌烦。派对、语言障碍，以及社交媒体上的那些高光时刻，真的会让人厌倦。

reference_translations.source_text: And so the purpose of this video, I want to talk about Moderation and sustainability, because we just talked about how some of those things, or at least the glamorous things, are not truly sustainable. So I want to present a business model that allows you to create your ideal lifestyle through practice, experimentation, and iteration.
reference_translations.cn_text: 所以这个视频的目的，我想谈谈适度和可持续性，因为我们刚刚谈到了其中一些事情，或者至少那些光鲜亮丽的事情，并不是真正可持续的。所以我想介绍一种商业模式，让你能够通过实践、实验和迭代来创造你理想的生活方式。

rewrite_cases.rewrite_prompt:
你是专业的中文配音文本改写专家。
